questions.q: Using the Binomial Theorem, expand (1 + x)^6 up to the term containing x^3.
questions.answer: Using the Binomial Theorem, we can expand (1 + x)^6 as follows: (1 + x)^6 = 1 + 6x + 15x^2 + 20x^3 + 15x^4 + 6x^5 + x^6. Hence, the term containing x^3 is 20x^3.
marking_scheme: Expansion of (1 + x)^6 up to the term containing x^3 (2 marks)
Correct power of x in the term (1 mark)

questions.q: Find the value of (1 - x)^(-4) using the Binomial Theorem.
questions.answer: Using the Binomial Theorem, we can expand (1 - x)^(-4) as follows: (1 - x)^(-4) = 1 + 4x + 6x^2 + 4x^3 + x^4 + ... . As the series is infinite, we can rewrite it as 1 + 4x + 6x^2 + 4x^3 + x^4 + ... = 1 + 4x (1 - x)^(-3). We know that (1 - x)^(-3) = 1 + 3x + 3x^2 + x^3 + ... . Hence, (1 - x)^(-4) = 1 + 4x (1 + 3x + 3x^2 + x^3 + ...).
marking_scheme: Expansion of (1 - x)^(-4) following the Binomial Theorem (2 marks)
Correct simplification of the series (1 mark)

questions.q: If a coin is tossed three times, find the probability of getting more than 2 heads using the Binomial Theorem.
questions.answer: When a coin is tossed three times, the total number of possible outcomes is 2^3 = 8. The possible outcomes are HHH, HHT, HTH, THH, HTT, THT, TTH, TTT. We want to find the probability of getting more than 2 heads, which means we want to find the probability of getting 3 heads. The probability of getting 3 heads is 1/8. Hence, the required probability is 1/8.
marking_scheme: Total number of possible outcomes (1 mark)
Possible outcomes with more than 2 heads (1 mark)

questions.q: Using the Binomial Theorem, expand (x + 1)^5 up to the term containing x^2.
questions.answer: Using the Binomial Theorem, we can expand (x + 1)^5 as follows: (x + 1)^5 = 1 + 5x + 10x^2 + 10x^3 + 5x^4 + x^5. Hence, the term containing x^2 is 10x^2.
marking_scheme: Expansion of (x + 1)^5 up to the term containing x^2 (2 marks)
Correct power of x in the term (1 mark)

questions.q: Find the value of (1 + 2x)^3 using the Binomial Theorem.
questions.answer: Using the Binomial Theorem, we can expand (1 + 2x)^3 as follows: (1 + 2x)^3 = 1 + 3(2x) + 3(2x)^2 + (2x)^3 = 1 + 6x + 12x^2 + 8x^3. Hence, the value of (1 + 2x)^3 is 1 + 6x + 12x^2 + 8x^3.
marking_scheme: Expansion of (1 + 2x)^3 following the Binomial Theorem (2 marks)
Correct simplification of the series (1 mark)
